description: Golden manifest for the claims fixture (claimflow, insurance claims processing, ~3.1k lines Python/Flask). Larger than courier (~3x) to test how the token saving scales. Derived directly from the codebase. Scheduled-job rules whose effect duplicates a stimulus rule (e.g. SLA escalation calls the same escalate path) are covered by-effect, not separately gated; notification-only jobs (stale-doc reminder, appeal-window-closed notice) make no state change and are not gated. Temporal trigger-type verification is a known gap for the (future) LLM-judge pass.
allowed_extra_entities_note: Supporting/cross-cutting entities a faithful spec may add beyond the domain entities: the adjuster actor and communication/audit entities. Any internal model entity not here and not an expected entity is flagged as a possible confabulation (review signal, not auto-fail).

ClaimDocument.note: Implicit state machine: no status column; state is derived from nullable timestamps requested_at/uploaded_at/verified_at/rejected_at. A distiller must invent the status enum; state_aliases lists accepted synonyms.

Policy.note: Mastered by a policy-admin system via webhook; read locally, never created by core flows.

rules.description: Claimant submits a claim (requires policy in force on incident date); Claim created in submitted.
Automated/manager triage: submitted -> triaged.
Assign to an adjuster: triaged -> investigating; creates a ReviewTask.
Escalate (manual or SLA-breach job, same effect): investigating -> escalated.
Approve a decidable claim. Cross-entity precondition (fraud clear + all documents verified + within policy coverage) is deliberately scattered across route/service/model.
Deny a decidable claim.
Approved claim -> settling; creates a pending Payment.
Pending payment -> issued (disbursement requested).
Issued payment clears -> cleared, and the claim becomes settled.
Pending payment fails -> failed (retried by sweep job, which creates a replacement payment).
Issued payment reversed -> reversed.
Claimant withdraws a non-terminal claim -> withdrawn.
Denied claim within the appeal window -> Appeal filed.
Filed appeal -> reviewing.
Appeal under review -> upheld (denial stands).
Appeal under review -> overturned; the claim reopens to investigating.
Requested document is uploaded.
Adjuster verifies an uploaded document.
Adjuster rejects an uploaded document.
A fraud check is requested for a claim; FraudCheck created pending.
Fraud-scoring webhook returns a verdict: pending -> clear or flagged. Fingerprinted on 'flagged' to avoid the cleared/clear substring clash; a clear verdict is what unblocks approval.
Open review task -> in_review.
In-review task -> resolved.
Active review task (open or in_review) -> cancelled.
A supporting document is requested from the claimant; ClaimDocument created in 'requested'.

boundaries_note: System boundaries a faithful distillation should expose as surfaces/actors. Matched fuzzily; informational, not gated.

exclusions.description: Dead-code traps and implementation details a faithful spec must omit.
